observations.recommendation:
1.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory
and Inspection Report on
Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) and to dispose all unserviceable
properties at the earliest possible time
pursuant to Section 79 of PD 1445 and Sections 379-381 of RA 7160.
Additionally, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor implement the One-
Time Cleansing guidelines prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020 and require the Inventory Committee to plan ahead the conduct
of Physical Inventory to be used in the One-Time Cleansing and ensure that the
RPCPPE will be submitted on time.
Furthermore, implement a regular assessment of the impairment of the
LGU’s assets in line with IPSAS.

2.7 We recommended that the LCE take immediate measures to ensure
procurement of comprehensive insurance coverage for all insurable
properties of the LGU, including the vehicles, infrastructure and other
valuable properties. The insurance coverage should be for fire, theft,
natural disasters and other relevant coverage based on the specific risks
faced by the LGU.

Likewise, we recommended that the Inventory Committee review the
existing Inventory List of PPE and identify those that are insurable and
would require insurance coverage. Ensure that the required PIF is
appropriately filled out, updated and accurate, and submitted to COA and
GSIS to facilitate proper insurance documentation.

3.20 We recommended the following measures to the Local Chief Executive to
ensure accurate representation and avoid payments more than the delivered
accomplishment:
a. Require the BAC and Engineer/MPDC to conduct inspection within 24 hours
upon receipt of the request from the Contractor and accurately reflect the
work accomplishment and date of the conduct of inspection in the report;
b. Require the Engineer to ensure that all inspections are conducted thoroughly
with appropriate documentation, including detailed pictures with timestamps,
geotagging, and precise description of the work completed;
c. Implement random cross-checks to verify physical completion and adherence
to project specifications; and
d. Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that payment releases are made
only for those sufficiently documented work accomplishments through
verified inspections and pictures, and to compute and impose liquidated
damages, if necessary.

4.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MPDC and
other officials concerned to:
c. Conduct early procurement planning, improve market studies, and
ensure compliance with RA 9184 to prevent procurement and
implementation delays.
d. Require periodic progress reports on fund utilization and project
implementation to facilitate proactive issue resolution.
Otherwise, identify alternative priority projects that can be implemented
within the fiscal year to optimize fund utilization.

5.8 We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure
that all disbursements are supported with complete documentation which
should include specific activity to which fund was used.

Further, we recommended that the LCE require the MDRRMO to observe the
timely submission of the LDRRMF Utilization Report prepared and certified
by the Accountant.

6.16 To address the inefficient utilization of the GAD fund and ensure that
gender-sensitive activities/programs are incorporated into LGU funded
projects, we recommend the following:
a. Formulate a gender-responsive budgeting framework to ensure that
gender considerations are incorporated at LGU implemented projects.
b. Establish coordination between the GAD Focal person/Office and other
departments concerned to ensure GAD integration into LGU funded
PPAs.
c. Implement a system for tracking and evaluating the GAD Fund
utilization.
d. Use the GAD Accomplishment Report Template set by the Philippine
Commission on Women.

7.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Engineer to post billboards in the project site on all implemented
infrastructure projects and/or to subsequently update the tarpaulin
signboards in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-
004.

Further, require the Accountant to ensure that the pictures of the posted
signboards be part of the supporting documents in the payment of progress
billings of infrastructure projects. Also, disclose appropriately the
complete information on the status of the Construction-In-Progress
account.

8.19 To address these concerns and to ensure that hiring practices adhere to the
principles of transparency, efficiency, and necessity, we recommend that the
LCE limit the hiring of Job Order workers to indispensable functions only
and pay the daily minimum wage prescribed by the DBM-CSC-COA JC No.
1, series of 2017 by employing the following measures:
a. Require the HRMO to determine the necessity, and assess the duties and
functions of JO workers, taking into account their expertise, experience,
and capability to handle the position to come up with the ideal number of
personnel to be hired;
b. Require the MPDC and HRMO to align JO hiring with actual workload
demands, ensuring that personnel are engaged only where there is a
legitimate need. Also, to develop a clear and equitable wage structure for
JO personnel to prevent inconsistencies and unjust compensation
practices.
c. Require all departments to submit detailed justifications for JO hiring,
including workload analysis and impact assessments.
d. Provide in the contract of service the specific details or information such
as expected output/service to be rendered, duration of work,
office/project assignment, source of funds, and its daily rate; and
e. Require each JO worker to prepare the accomplishment report, stating
therein the actual and specific tasks undertaken during the period, duly
verified and accepted by the immediate supervisor.

9.9 We recommended that the LGU's HDMF contribution share be
appropriately provided for in the appropriation of the LGU Funds for the
JO Employees mandated to be enrolled in the HDMF Membership.
Henceforth, ensure that the LGU withholds and remits the required
contribution, including the LGU share, to the HDMF
Henceforth, we recommended that the LGU request HDMF to conduct an
orientation session for the HR and/or Payroll Employees to ensure full
understanding and compliance with HDMF membership requirements
and information dissemination with covered employees.

10.10 We recommended that Local Chief Executive should ensure that any
Lakbay Aral conducted and/or conferences/conventions attended by
Official and Employees are responsive to the organizational result and
development outcome, keeping in mind the prudent use of the scarce
financial resources of the LGU.

Henceforth, traveling of similar nature should start with a documented
needs assessment to ensure that the intervention is addressing an actual
need and are aligned with the local development goals/plans. Further, after
every travel require the participating official/employee to submit a post-
activity report on the practices observed, lessons learned and how these
practices can be implemented or applied in the LGU.

11.11 We recommended that the Local School Board be actively involved in the
SEF budget preparation process by ensuring that the budget preparation
timelines are sufficiently communicated with all the members of the LSB
to provide them ample time to review and provide inputs on the proposed
budget.

Likewise, periodically review the composition of the LSB to ensure that it
continuously reflects the changing organizational set-up or required
representatives, particularly the representation of the youth or the SK.

Furthermore, facilitate regular consultations and forums from key
stakeholders, particularly the teachers, parents, and students to encourage
inputs in the identification of the programmed activities/projects to ensure
that the SEF budget aligns with the educational needs of the District.

12.10 We recommended that the Local School Board establish a monitoring
scheme that would regularly assess the utilization of the fund to ensure that
SEF allocations are effectively utilized and all disbursements for the
education sector are aligned with the objectives of the LGU and the SEF
for the enhancement of the education programs for the
District/Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the timely submission of the Financial Statements and their related schedules and observed the documentation to facilitate the prompt verification and adjustments of any errors/misclassifications.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the FS in CY 2023, henceforth, observed the documentation to facilitate timely submission of monthly and annual FS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to conduct regular inventory, inspection, and assessment of all the properties of the LGU which should include the identification of the unserviceable PPE and proposal of the appropriate mode of disposal.
observations.managementAction: The inventory committee conducted the inventory inspection and reconciled the report with the Accounting and Treasurer.
observations.reason: Absence of records of the proposal of the MV and Constraint on human resources and technical know-how on the assessment of the PPE

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Budget Officer and Accountant to maintain their record of the appropriation and utilization of the fund. Further, ensure that the minimum budgetary and reporting requirements are complied with to provide accurate information on any amount that may be subjected to re-appropriation by the LDRRM Council and Sangguniang Bayan.
observations.managementAction: The LDRMF Reports were grouped and classified per thematic area. Also, the LDRRM appropriations were implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Officials concerned coordinate and implement measures and improve the storage environment and facilitate tracking, monitoring, and replenishment of stocks of the LGU that may be needed in its operation.
observations.managementAction: The RHU sorted out their supplies and equipment and properly stored. However, the LDRRM inventories is yet to be transferred to the newly constructed storage.
observations.reason: Not implemented: Absence of records of the MRF to inspect and oversee the management of barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE create a monitoring team to conduct regular checking and evaluation of the status/condition of the infrastructure projects of the LGU and recommend appropriate actions for its maintenance and/or repairs. In the case of structural defects and deficiency, require that the contractor be required to immediately effect the necessary repair/restoration works considering that it is still within the defect liability period and/or warranty period.
observations.managementAction: LPMC was created. Further, the contractor was required to immediately effect the necessary repair/restoration works considering the defect liability period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE take the following courses of action to ensure the effective implementation of the DRRM Program: a. Regularly review the annual LDRRM Plan, evaluate the realization of the programmed PPAs, and revise or recommend for re-appropriation; b. Expressly include the GAD aspect in the LDRRM Plan and Budget; c. Establish a monitoring and evaluation scheme on the implementation of the DRRM PPAs; and d. Ensure that the minimum budgetary and reporting requirements are complied with.
observations.managementAction: The LDRMF Reports were grouped and classified per thematic area. Infrastructure appropriations were identified to be longer implementable and were re-appropriated to other priority PPAs eligible to be funded out of the DRRM Fund. GAD sensitive components were incorporated in the LDRRM Infrastructure. Minimum budgetary and reporting requirements are being complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Solid Waste Management Board through the Local Chief Executive require all barangays to establish an operational MRF. Likewise, conduct regular inspection and monitoring of the established MRFs to ensure the operationalization of these facilities including waste diversion and effective record management. Further, enforce strict implementation of the sanctions provided in the Municipal ordinances and RA 9003 against individuals and entities that continuously violate the prohibited acts thereof.
observations.managementAction: Conducted various discussions with Barangay Officials and monitored the implementation of the establishment of MRFs. Further, requested barangays to coordinate and implement SWM within the barangay.
observations.reason: Not Implemented: Lack of MENRO personnel to inspect and oversee the management of barangays.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive to: a. Instruct the MENRO to look into the disposal and segregation of controlled wastes in the Open Disposal Facility and Residual Containment Area operated by LGU; b. Require the Solid Waste Management Board to devise measures consistent with the provisions of R.A 9003 and its IRR to prevent hazards or risks posed to the health of residents, the garbage collectors and the environment; c. Evaluate the suitability of the current location of the dumpsite and containment area and consider relocation; d. Implement comprehensive monitoring and review processes on the collection and disposal of waste to ensure adherence to waste management regulations and standards and continuous improvement in its practices.
observations.managementAction: A MENRO was designated to focus on the SWM initiatives. The Barangay Officials were requested to coordinate and implement SWM within the barangay. A lot was rented to operate as temporary disposal site. A taskforce was created to oversee segregation at source and monitor the implementation of the Solid Waste Management Programs.
observations.reason: Not Implemented: Absence of sufficient appropriation. Also, the LGU has no MENRO to focus on the SWM Program.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive stop the grant of representation allowance and honoraria to national government employees until the receipt of the Municipality has fully implemented the Modified Salary Standardization Schedule and other conditions prescribed in the Salary Standardization Law.
observations.managementAction: Stopped the grant of representation allowance and honoraria to NGA Employees upon observing the minimum requirements for NGA Employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that National Government Agency traveling employees were duly authorized by their mother agency to go on travel and permitted to claim the traveling expenses from the LGU. Henceforth, ensure that all travel orders and itineraries of travel shall be approved by their respective authorized official. Further, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive refrain from approving/allowing assistance that is not within the Municipality’s responsibility to finance and/or subsidize.
observations.managementAction: Observed the minimum requirement for traveling expenses even for NGA Employees. Food allowance and/or incentives were no longer granted unless necessary.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the human resource management officer to strictly observe the prescribed minimum and not approve certifications to employees for loans that would result to monthly net take-home pay less than the minimum net take home pay.
observations.managementAction: Certifications to employees for loans were no longer approved if found that the amortization would result to less than the minimum net take home pay.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to ensure the collection of amounts from the persons liable for the settlement of the disallowed transactions prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-06.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant requested for the copy of the ND so as to appropriately deduct the disallowed amount from the employee concerned.
observations.reason: Not Implemented: Absence of a copy of the list of the disallowed employees/ payees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to immediately submit to the Audit Team the missing documents relative to the Notices of Suspension to avoid disallowance in audit pursuant to Section 82 of Presidential Decree 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant requested for the list of the suspended transactions and is still in the process of retrieving the files of the NS, suspended transactions. The Municipal Treasurer will submit missing documents relative to the Notices of Suspension.
observations.reason: Not Implemented: Absence of the list of the suspended transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Chief Executive instruct the: a. Engineering Office to adequately conduct the detailed engineering and ensure that the PERT/CPM should include all the holidays and other instances that could adversely affect the construction works; b. Municipal Engineer to validate the facts and extent of delay before recommending the grant of work suspension/extension of contract time; and c. Municipal Engineer/MPDC to regularly monitor/conduct ocular inspection on the projects during the construction period to appropriately assess the performance of the contractors in the entire duration of the infrastructure works.
observations.managementAction: The detailed engineering was prepared before any procurement of infrastructure project. The Monitoring Committee was created. Further, the Engineer regularly conducts inspection and validation of on-going projects. The checklist of the minimum supporting documents shall be attached to the claims for payment to ensure that documentation is adequately complied.
observations.reason: Not Implemented: Insufficient manpower in the Engineering Office who was also the MPDC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following measures to the Local Chief Executive to ensure the effective implementation of the infrastructure projects: a. Create a Local Monitoring Committee whose function shall include the monitoring of the implementation of the PPAs of the LGUs, from procurement to execution/construction, and payment; b. Instruct the Municipal Engineer/MPDC and BAC to conduct extensive monitoring on the implementation of the constructed PPAs and to appropriately communicate all findings found during inspection which shall also include the actual percentage of completion so that the contractor can immediately request payment in accordance with the IRR of RA 9184; c. Require the BAC and Engineer/MPDC to conduct an inspection within 24 hours upon receipt of the request from the Contractor and accurately reflect the work accomplishment and date of the conduct of inspection in the report; and d. Require the Monitoring Committee, Engineer, and BAC to sufficiently inform the LCE of the result of the inspection and the Accountant to facilitate the payment accordingly and/or effect any deductions for liquidated damages or other sanctions thereto.
observations.managementAction: LPMC was implemented. Create MPDC Monitoring Team to intensify the monitoring of implementation of infrastructure projects. Engineer inspects all projects. Accountant implemented requirement to submit picture of the infra project as of the certification date so as to ensure that the billing reconciles with the percentage of SWA.
observations.reason: Not Implemented: Insufficient manpower in the Implementing offices to conduct timely inspections; delayed submission of project status of PPAs.

observations.recommendation: Prepare the Capacity Development Agenda and the Local Chief Executive should ensure that seminars and conventions attended is responsive to the organizational result and development outcome, keeping in mind the prudent use of the scarce financial resources of the LGU. Also, before approving the authority to attend any seminar-workshop the authority and competencies of the sponsoring Government organization/agency should be accordingly verified.
observations.managementAction: The LGU prepared the CapDev. The HRMO records and monitors the seminars/training attended by all the LGU Officials/Employees. All requests for travel are reviewed by the HRMO to ensure there were no duplications. Attendance to Seminars sponsored by Government Agencies is mandatory.
observations.reason: Delayed preparation of the CapDev. Lack of personnel in the HRMO to prepare the database of the training needs and attended seminars.

observations.recommendation: We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure that an ICS was issued to the end-user and appropriately recorded in the stock cards to establish accountability and monitor its whereabouts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to: a. require the Municipal Accountant to submit the supporting details/schedules and to provide adequate and necessary disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements;

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):

requests for purchase of properties/equipment;
b. Take over the contract or award the contract to a qualified contractor
through negotiation and forfeit the performance security;

unperformed works for the motorboat and impose other administrative
sanctions and remedies pursuant to Rule XXIII of RA 9184;
d. Require the Inspection Officer to issue a revised Inspection Report to
reflect the actual status of the motorboat;
e. Instruct the Property Custodian to ensure that only the deliveries that are in
accordance with the contract shall be accepted;
f. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to verify/validate the reliability of the
Certificates of Completion/Inspection before certifying the completeness of the
supporting documents submitted for payment, and
g. Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to carefully examine the
specifications of the requests submitted for bidding/procurement and the
Technical and Financial Proposals submitted by the interested bidder as
required in R.A. 9184 and its Revised IRR.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer to exercise due diligence in securing the LGU funds. Also, require
the Municipal Treasurer to refund the bank charges totaling ₱72,600.00.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to procure consultancy
services pursuant to the provisions in Republic Act No. 9184.
b. Require the Human Resource Management Officer to observe the rules
and regulations on the hiring of consultants and to assess the necessity of the
services of consultant in order to avoid unnecessary expenditure of government
funds, and evaluate whether the responsibilities of the existing consultant
duplicate those of the regular employees.
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the required documents to
support payments to the consultant.
d. Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that payments for
consultancy services are supported with complete documentation.
This audit recommendation is without prejudice to issuance of Notice of
Suspension or Disallowance.

for the immediate release of AICS.
b. Instruct the MSWDO to prioritize the preparation of the assessment
report and immediately submit to the concerned officers to facilitate the
outright release of the AICS.
c. Coordinate with the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office
in formulating the implementing guidelines to regulate the financial aid
or assistance granted to various individuals in crisis situation aligned
with the DSWD-Memorandum Circular No. 02 S of 2014.
d. Instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office and
Municipal Accountant to ensure that all AICS are supported with
complete supporting documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor install control and monitoring
mechanisms to ensure that the use of government vehicles and consumption
of fuel shall strictly be for a valid public purpose. Henceforth, the following
documents and reports should be required on the use of government
vehicles and fuel consumption:
a) Driver’s Trip Tickets shall be serially numbered and approved by the
Municipal Mayor or his authorized representative to control the use of
government vehicle;
b) Monthly Report of Official Travels prepared by the driver concerned
and Monthly Equipment Utilization Report prepared by the equipment
custodian summarizing in chronological order his trips or use of
equipment, respectively, for the month, duly certified by the driver and
approved by the Administrative Officer; and
c) Report of Fuel Consumption shall be prepared and submitted monthly
to the auditor for verification purposes. The report shall show the
different types of motor vehicles utilized by the agency during the
month, total distance traveled, total fuel consumed and the normal fuel
consumption for each vehicle.

undertaken for the budget year, with details on the target number of
participants, number of days, and estimated cost per pax/day, and
submit to the Head of BAC Secretariat for consolidation. Supplemental
APP should also be requested, if necessary;
b. Bids and Awards Committee to submit a justification on the deficiencies
observed in the mode of procurement and submit the technical
documents of the supplier. Also, submit the Purchase Orders/Contracts
of Service on its ensuing transactions together with other technical
documents required in COA Circular 2009-001 to the Audit Team
within 5 days from execution; and
c. The Municipal Accountant to strictly review financial transactions
before certifying as to the completeness of the claim, in compliance with
COA Circular 2012-001. Moreover, require the submission of the
lacking supporting documents such as the Program/Activity Proposal,
menu, pictures and the supplier’s/caterer’s documents to the audit team
immediately.
This audit recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Suspension/Disallowance as a result of our audit.

a. Direct the Bids and Awards Committee to evaluate the eligibilities of the
bidder to ensure that all the procurement of the LGU are awarded to
contractors who can satisfactorily perform the obligations required
under the contract.
b. The MPDC and Municipal Engineer to intensify the monitoring and
coordination of all on-going projects to minimize defects and ensure that
the progress of construction are within the timetable provided in the
agreed construction works.
c. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to immediately inspect the RHU and
prepare the computation of liquidated damages.
d. Enforce the sanctions and penalties to defaulting contractors, such as
imposition of administrative suspension for one to two years from
participating in all the government bidding process, termination of the
contract, forfeiture of the performance security and enforcement of the
liquidated damages, as provided for in Republic Act 9184.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:
a. The Municipal Engineer to create a well-planned detailed engineering
and adequately review and evaluate program of works in all aspect of
the construction/phases of the project to ensure that the POW shall
cover at least a usable portion of the project and no construction shall
be started for portions of the project that are less than usable;
b. The MPDC to identify the reasons and address the hindrances in the
commencement of the additional phases of construction for the project;
c. The Municipal Engineer to prepare and submit the Program of Work
(POW), plans/drawings and technical specification with detailed
breakdown of the cost up to the completion of the project, separate from
the POW per phases, so as to appropriately allocate funds and to ensure
continuity of the works.

We recommended that the MPDC, MENRO and Municipal Engineer
coordinate and adequately plan the procurement of infrastructure projects.
Strategic locations of each type of infrastructure projects should be
considered, including the assessment of the access road to the structures and
the protection of public health and environment. Meanwhile, alternative
measures and/or use of either the isolation facility or the MRF should be
formulated so as not to put LGU funds to waste.

We recommended the following to the Municipal Mayor:
a) Instruct the members of MIATF to ensure that environmental surfaces
in the quarantine facility are regularly and thoroughly cleaned,
sanitized and maintained before, during and after the quarantine period
of the occupants.
b) Install adequate and appropriate storage area for the COVID-19 related
property and equipment.
c) Recommend the repair of the boat ambulance and other alternative on
how to salvage its use and part, if repair is no longer economical.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the deficiencies,
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009, and Sections 111 and 112 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Create an Inventory Committee that will conduct complete annual physical
inventory-taking of PPE using the guidelines enumerated in COA Circular
2020-006, and submit a copy of the RPCPPE/Inventory Report not later than
January 31 of each year using the prescribe format in Annex A of the Circular;
b. Require the General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant to update property records based on the procedural guidelines
provided in the same Circular to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE
accounts to be considered as the correct balance of the LGU’s PPE; and
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to comply with the guidelines on
disclosures, and derecognizing PPE accounts that are no longer existing, and
prepare adjusting journal entries to correct the erroneous accounting entries.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a) Municipal Accountant and GSO/Municipal Treasurer to record regular
purchases of inventories using the perpetual inventory method;
b) GSO/Municipal Treasurer to conduct regular count of inventories on
hand; Prepare the required inventory reports, schedules, and stock cards;
Use/accomplish the prescribed inventory forms;
c) Municipal Accountant to ensure coordination between the end-users and
the accounting office for the submission of required reports and the proper
accounting for the inventories; and
d) Municipal Accountant to ensure proper recording and posting of financial
transactions to the general ledger and supplies ledger cards to come up
with the correct totals at the end of the year as reported balance in the
financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
provide additional information to all accounts in the notes, especially those
showing significant increase/decrease. Likewise, the notes should also indicate the
nature, composition or any changes thereto pursuant to the provisions of IPSAS
Volume I and Section 111 of PD 1445, to provide readers with the information
necessary for the full understanding of the operation of the LGU.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive and other concerned local officials to conduct an annual physical count of its Property, Plant and Equipment as well as the departments concerned to maintain records for the reconciliation of the account pursuant to NGAS Manual, hence it will contribute to the reliability of the account in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to strictly observe the timely submission of accounts and financial reports in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: AAR Submitted some of the paid DVs, Official Receipts and financial reports.
observations.reason: Late submission of DV’s, Official Receipts and financial reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer be required to submit copies of approved/perfected contracts with the required documents within 5 days after their execution/issuance and copies of delivery documents within 24 hours after acceptance of delivered goods and services as provided for in COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 2009-002.
observations.managementAction: Management will comply to submit all perfected contracts with the required documents.
observations.reason: Management were not aware on the COA Circular No. 2009-001 and 2009-002; submission of copies of contracts after execution and acceptance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit the lacking documents to support the disbursements. This is without prejudice to the result of post-audit once the documents are submitted.
observations.managementAction: AAR Submitted some of the lacking required documents to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management continue the conduct of intensive Tax Campaign in the barangays; deploy personnel/staff/collectors for collection; and look into the causes of the decrease in income in order to improve operations.
observations.managementAction: Continue the conduct of intensive Tax Campaign in the barangays. Deployment of staff/collectors for collection.
observations.reason: Due to other office related tasks that needs immediate action. Lack of personnel in the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management procure common-use supplies from the Procurement Service (PS) Depot as mandated by AO No. 17 and DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6. Common-use supplies not available at the PS Depot should be procured in accordance with the IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management explained procurement resorted due to necessity of the equipment only at the service depot.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prepare a Gender and Development (GAD) Plan and appropriate at least 5% of the total budget appropriation for GAD pursuant to DBM, NEDA and NCRFW Joint Memorandum Circular 2004-1 dated April 5, 2004.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to provide funding in the annual budget for GAD this ensuing year.
observations.reason: Amount appropriated was below the minimum requirement of 5% of the total budget due to insufficient fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer should prepare the monthly CRAAF and a copy be submitted to the auditor. The balances of unused accountable forms should be thoroughly verified to determine whether all issued accountable forms actually are duly reported.
observations.managementAction: The ICO-Municipal Treasurer is doing his best to be able to prepare and submit the monthly Consolidated Report of Accountability of Accountable Forms (CRAAF).
observations.reason: Lack of personnel in the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management insure its insurable properties with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS to secure the agency from acts of nature, accidents and man-made calamities and protect it from possible economic setback.
observations.managementAction: Management had complied partially to prepare and submit insurance of properties of the municipalities.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel and insufficient budget.
